figures.caption: Figure 6. Additional visualizations of synthetic samples generated by our data construction pipeline.
Figure 4. Visualization of the synthetic data generated though our data generation pipeline.

Figure 3. Comparison of our result with other baseline models on WISA-test set. Results show that our method shows better understanding of the physics law, which demostrates our method’s strong generalization ability to act as real-world simulator. Zoom-in for details.
Figure 5. The visualization of our user study page.

Figure 2. PhysAlign framework. Our data generation pipeline leverages physical simulator (i.e. Blender) to generate synthetic videos with 3D physical ground truth. Our method aligns the DiT [32] latent features with both (i) physical knowledge feature by V-JEPA2 [1], and (ii) 3D geometric feature encoded from synthetic ground truth (e.g., depth). This unified alignment internalizes both physical laws and visual fidelity for I2V generation task.
Table 4. VBench-I2V evaluation on the WISA-test set. Higher is better.

Table 3. VBench-I2V evaluation on the paired-subject set. Higher is better. I2V-Metrics: i2v subject (subject consistency between input image and video), i2v background (background consistency). Quality Metrics: subj consis. (temporal subject consistency), bkg consis. (temporal background consistency), motion smooth. (motion smoothness), dynamic degree (motion amplitude), aesthetic (aesthetic quality), imaging (imaging quality).
